My biggest frustration is with audiobooks—they don't always resume where I left off, making it hard to find my place again. Overall, it's a great streaming service with excellent music discovery, but there's room for improvement.

Spotify has been my preferred music service for years, but the frequent price increases are unreasonable. Stop gouging your loyal customers to add unnecessary features we're not interested in -- Spotify isn't a book store or a video hub, stop trying to do what your competitors have been doing better for years. original_review: This app is underwhelming. If the current experience were a launch, it would feel solid. I'd be optimistic for the team to smooth over bad UX and bugs over time. Unfortunately the timeline is reversed: almost every interaction has noticeable delays, the "new" widget seems to spin or show the wrong track ~half the time I actually want to use it, common tasks take many clicks (exacerbated by lag), and none of this was true 10 years ago when I last reviewed the app. Functional but disappointing.

It seems any recorded artist on planet earth-since the beginning of sound recordings-can be found on Spotify.  It doesn’t get any better. Not only music, but books and information of incredible interest.

original_review: The application is completely broken! Everything was fine before — I calmly listened to music, went to all kinds of parties, and listened to tracks on my way. The subtitles were great, and there was a lot of free choice. But everything broke down when the developers decided to add the smart shuffle function and ruined everything! It is impossible to listen to your playlist normally. You want to turn it off? Only with premium. You can't go back to the beginning of the song, you can't switch to the next one, and you can't rewind music to a certain point. You can't do anything! I used to think it was a great app, but now it's absolute trash.

original_review:
I pay for this. I should not hit shuffle only for it to do the same exact “shuffled” songs every single time. I should not have to delete my cache daily just for ONE instance of it not playing the same songs before it goes back to the exact shuffle. I’ve redownloaded the app, cleared the cache, redownloaded the playlists, it’s the same every time. I’m not the only one dealing with this either. 

There’s multiple posts on Reddit about it with clearing the cache being the only temporary solution. 

On top of that, lately, when I pause and go to look at the song or switch, the app is paused at the begging of a DIFFERENT song than what I know I just paused in the MIDDLE of. 

Fix this please, otherwise I’m just going back to downloading songs on my phone and using a shuffler app. Spotify is convenient but it is not the only place I can go to.

original_review: It is unfortunate to see how Spotify has shifted to keep all the song selecting freedom to premium packs. Many of the users started using Spotify because it was so amazing to just have a player play whatever you want, without any nonsense. The way it annoys the hell out of the user, when you want to just listen to one song and try a million playlists to not get it at all, is such a de-motivator. I loved using this app before all the premium annoyance. So, I just go back to YouTube mostly. People don’t listen to music just to have some noise in their ears, it’s a way to give way to one’s emotions! Sometimes it’s about nostalgia, sometimes about lost love, sometimes family and what not! I wish someone in this world cared about keeping the things the way they were! That would actually create a loyal consumer base, even if money is all you can think about!
